escalatedRisks.risk: Samsung bonus resentment → HBM packaging intentional production slowdowns 🆕
escalatedRisks.status: NEW — Day 1. More insidious than strike — 'work negligence, canceled meetings, intentional slowdowns' in TSP divisions. No clear endpoint.
escalatedRisks.source: Tom's Hardware / Strait Times / StartupFortune / Investing.com May 27-28 2026
escalatedRisks.detail: Newly ratified agreement creates DS (semiconductor) $400K vs DX (consumer) $4K bonus disparity (100:1). Resentment has spread to TSP (Test & Package) divisions — reports of 'work negligence,' 'canceled meetings,' and 'intentional production slowdowns.' HBM delivery schedules impacted. Packaging capacity shared with enterprise SSD → indirect risk. Unlike strike (can be ended by agreement), organizational resentment has no clear resolution mechanism.
escalatedRisks.confidence: medium-high (multi-source qualitative, no quantitative impact data yet)

escalatedRisks.detail: Mouser U.2 61.44TB only 1 unit for 9th consecutive day. 4 on order Sept 7. BUT newly discovered alternative channels: Connection.com In Stock 1-2 day ship $33,047 + GovConnection In Stock 24-48hr $33,213 + CDW 8-10 day ETA. Channel premium 34% vs Newegg $24,600 reflects supply tightness not total unavailability. 2026 Q3/Q4 QLC procurement: viable through Connection/CDW at premium pricing. Crisis urgency downgraded from extreme to high.

escalatedRisks.risk: NAND Q1 contract revised UP to +85-90% (was +70-75%) 🆕
escalatedRisks.status: NEW — Day 1. Upward revision from TrendForce 5/25. Top 5 NAND Q1 revenue +83.7% QoQ.
escalatedRisks.detail: NAND flash contract prices climbed +85-90% QoQ in Q1 2026, exceeding previous estimates of +70-75%. Top 5 suppliers combined revenue +83.7% QoQ. This means upstream NAND pricing was more aggressive than previously estimated. Q2 +70-75% still projected. Implies PCIe4 TLC tracking lag may be worse than current 27-155% estimate. 5-year SSD LTA becoming industry standard supports structural shortage thesis.

escalatedRisks.risk: DDR5 profitability surpassing HBM3e → capacity reallocation 🆕
escalatedRisks.status: NEW — Day 1. Medium-term positive for DDR5 supply, negative for HBM pricing.
escalatedRisks.detail: TrendForce projects DDR5 profitability will exceed HBM3e starting Q1 2026. HBM3e/DDR5 ASP gap narrowing from 4-5x to 1-2x by end-2026. Suppliers may reallocate wafers from HBM to server DDR5 to capture higher profitability. Structurally positive for DDR5 supply in H2 2026-2027. Creates upward pricing pressure on HBM as supply contracts.
escalatedRisks.confidence: medium (TrendForce projection, actual reallocation pace and magnitude unknown)

escalatedRisks.risk: Samsung union vote ratified — strike risk CLOSED 🔴
escalatedRisks.status: CLOSED — May 27, 2026. 73.7% approval, 95.5% turnout. BUT bonus resentment creates new HBM packaging slowdown risk.
escalatedRisks.source: Korea JoongAng Daily / Korea Herald / Chosun Ilbo / Tom's Hardware May 27 2026
escalatedRisks.detail: Samsung's largest union ratified the tentative agreement. 62,616 of 65,593 eligible members voted (95.5%). 46,142 YES votes (73.7%). SGUU (DS-heavy) 80.6%. NSEU (DX-heavy) 21.1%. DS employees get 10.5% operating profit in stock bonuses (~$340K-$540K/person). DX employees get flat 6M won (~$4,000). Strike risk formally eliminated. Samsung warm-down → ramp-up recovery 2-3 weeks (mid-June, possibly delayed by packaging friction). New risk: bonus resentment causing HBM packaging intentional slowdowns.

escalatedRisks.risk: 5-year SSD/HDD LTA becoming industry standard 🆕
escalatedRisks.status: NEW — Day 1. Structural supply constraint signal — large buyers locking supply through 2030-2031.
escalatedRisks.detail: Crushing shortages push customers to sign record 5-year LTAs for SSDs and HDDs. Hyperscalers and cloud providers securing capacity through 2031. Implication: small/medium buyers face increasing difficulty accessing spot supply. Structural market change — not temporary.

escalatedRisks.risk: Samsung HBM5E production indefinitely postponed
escalatedRisks.status: NEW -- D1d (7th Gen 10nm) DRAM yield shortfall. Samsung HBM roadmap facing dual technology+organizational obstacles.
escalatedRisks.detail: Samsung indefinitely postponed HBM5E mass production due to insufficient D1d DRAM yields. Follows earlier HBM4 delays from 1c DRAM yield issues. Combined with TSP slowdowns, Samsung faces compounding HBM challenges. SK Hynix widens lead. DDR5 capacity may benefit from reduced HBM competition for advanced nodes.

escalatedRisks.risk: Dell AI server revenue raised to $60B
escalatedRisks.status: NEW -- FY2027 guidance +20% from $50B. AI data center buildout accelerating.
escalatedRisks.detail: Dell raised AI server revenue to ~$60B (from $50B). Top-5 CSPs $770B+ AI capex (+87% YoY). Demand-side pressure on DDR5 and enterprise SSD is structural and growing.

escalatedRisks.risk: NAND Q3 2026 full-year sustained price increases confirmed
escalatedRisks.status: NEW -- TrendForce confirms no new NAND capacity in 2026, prices sustained through Q3 and full year.
escalatedRisks.detail: NAND contract prices confirmed rising through Q3 2026. All major suppliers adding virtually no new production capacity. HDD shortage redirecting orders to QLC SSDs. Combined with Q1+85-90% and Q2+70-75%, sustained upward trajectory through year-end.

escalatedRisks.risk: DDR4 spot rebounds — DDR4/DDR5 concurrent rally = full DRAM bull market 🆕
escalatedRisks.status: ACCELERATING — Day 4 concurrent. DDR4 16Gb +0.58% to $61.952 / 8Gb +0.89% to $34.100 on May 29. DDR4/DDR5 concurrent rally entering 3rd week — no longer a brief technical bounce but structural bull market widening.
escalatedRisks.detail: DDR4 spot rebounded after consistent decline while DDR5 rose. Could signal DDR4 demand bottom found, rotational buying from DDR5, or broader DRAM market tightness extending to legacy nodes.

escalatedRisks.risk: DDR4 8Gb PC contract breaks $20 (+25% MoM) 🆕🆕 — DRAM full bull chain complete
escalatedRisks.status: NEW MILESTONE — May 2026. DDR4 8Gb PC contract avg $20.00, +25% MoM. DDR5 spot→DDR5 contract→DDR4 spot→DDR4 contract — full chain of DRAM price increases now complete with no gaps.
escalatedRisks.detail: General-purpose DDR4 8Gb PC contract price recorded $20.00 average for May 2026, representing +25% MoM increase. This is the first time DDR4 contract has broken the $20 psychological barrier. Combined with DDR5 spot 4th consecutive ATH ($41.900), DDR4 spot accelerating ($34.100), and DDR5 contract Q1+110-115%, the full DRAM bull market chain has been validated from spot to contract, high-end to low-end. DDR4 substitution path closing.
categoriesAffected: All DDR (broadening — DDR4 substitution path closing)
